text: Last class the partner said the price was final, and Daniel told me the deal would close.
Today you take the chair. I suggest moving on, and I propose to vote on the deal.

Good morning, everyone. I will facilitate today. The objective is to approve the plan. We have apologies from two attendees.
Thank you, Simone. I propose to vote on the plan at the end, after we hear from everyone.
Good idea. Let us allocate ten minutes to each item, and let us avoid going over time. I suggest moving on to the plan now.
The plan looks strong. I recommend keeping the same timeline, but we need to allocate more budget to the second phase.
Noted. We all agreed to proceed with the plan and add budget to phase two. Let me summarize the decisions before we close.
Clear summary. If there is nothing else, you can adjourn. Well chaired, Simone.
The objective today is to approve the plan.

Hi Simone, this is Daniel from the legal team, with a quick briefing before the meeting you will chair on Monday. The objective is to approve the project plan and the budget. We have apologies from two attendees, so they cannot come. I suggest allocating ten minutes to each item on the agenda, and I recommend avoiding going over time on the first topic, because it always takes longer than people expect. Invite each attendee to speak in turn, and when the team is ready, propose to vote on the plan. At the end, please summarize the decisions, agree on the next steps with the partner, and then adjourn the meeting. You will facilitate it very well. Good luck, and let me know how it goes.

First, the chair opens the meeting and states the objective and the apologies. Next, the chair suggests allocating time to each item. Then, during the discussion, the chair invites each attendee to speak, avoids going over time, and proposes to vote when the team is ready to proceed. After that, the team agrees to proceed with the plan. Finally, the chair summarizes the decisions, confirms the next steps, and adjourns the meeting.
